name: Sierra del Rincón – circular by Puebla de la Sierra desde Manjirón
name_original: Sierra del Rincón – circular por Puebla de la Sierra desde Manjirón
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Puentes Viejas navigate a landscape characterized by significant reservoirs, river valleys, and hilly terrain. The region features prominent water bodies like the Embalse del Atazar and El Villar Reservoir, often accompanied by gorges and forested areas. Elevations can be substantial, particularly in areas like the Sierra del Rincón, offering varied cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Puentes Viejas The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra del Rincón – circular by Puebla de la Sierra desde Manjirón, a 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours to complete, offering a challenging circular journey through the Sierra del Rincón. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse del Atazar – complete loop from El Berrueco – gravel, a difficult 37.1 miles (59.7 km) path. This route features gravel sections around the Embalse del Atazar, providing views of the reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Lozoyuela, a 42.9 miles (69.1 km) trail leading through the El Villar Reservoir and Gorge, often completed in about 5 hours. Touring cycling around Puentes Viejas is defined by expansive reservoirs, deep gorges, and the surrounding hilly terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult climbs. The routes in Puentes Viejas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puentes Viejas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Puentes Viejas?
A: There are nearly 100 touring cycling routes mapped around Puentes Viejas on komoot. The majority of these, about 70%, are classified as difficult, offering significant challenges for experienced cyclists. You'll also find a good selection of moderate routes and a few easier options.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Puentes Viejas?
A: While most routes in Puentes Viejas are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. These easier options typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the best routes for advanced touring cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Advanced touring cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962355949/sierra-del-rincon-circular-by-puebla-de-la-sierra-desde-manjiron" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Rincón – circular by Puebla de la Sierra desde Manjirón&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.2 km route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959844782/embalse-del-atazar-complete-loop-from-el-berrueco-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Atazar – complete loop from El Berrueco – gravel&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 59.7 km and includes gravel sections around the reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Puentes Viejas area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Puentes Viejas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Puerto de la Puebla loop from Lozoyuela&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, covering 69.1 km through scenic reservoir and gorge landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes around Puentes Viejas?
A: The routes in Puentes Viejas offer diverse scenery, characterized by significant reservoirs like Embalse del Atazar and El Villar, deep river valleys, and hilly terrain. You'll often cycle alongside water bodies, through forested areas, and encounter substantial elevations, especially in regions like the Sierra del Rincón.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks to see along the routes?
A: Along your rides, you can explore several notable landmarks and natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature. You might also encounter the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4606981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patones de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam and Cliffside Pathways&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vultures' Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Puentes Viejas?
A: The touring cycling routes in Puentes Viejas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.75 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the sense of accomplishment after completing the demanding climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Lozoya River?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide excellent views of the Lozoya River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14887857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from El Berrueco&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for this, combining river scenery with the expansive El Atazar Reservoir.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Puentes Viejas?
A: Given the hilly terrain and potential for significant elevation, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Puentes Viejas. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and sometimes snow in higher areas.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Puentes Viejas?
A: Many routes in Puentes Viejas start from villages or towns that offer public parking facilities. For example, routes often begin in places like El Berrueco or Lozoyuela, where you can typically find parking near the starting points. It's always advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended parking locations.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: While the routes often traverse natural and sometimes remote areas, many pass through or near small villages where you can find cafes, bars, or small shops for refreshments. Planning your route to include stops in towns like Puebla de la Sierra, El Berrueco, or Lozoyuela will ensure you have opportunities for breaks and to refuel.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Puentes Viejas?
A: Due to the varied terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, it's recommended to wear layers that can be adjusted to changing temperatures. A windproof and waterproof jacket is advisable, especially for higher elevations or unexpected weather. Comfortable cycling shorts, a helmet, and appropriate footwear are essential. Don't forget sun protection, even on cloudy days.
tips: The Puentes Viejas reservoir was built on the Lozoya River and is part of the water supply network known as Canal de Isabel II, which is still in operation after several centuries in use. It is also a panoramic site with very nice views.

Prádena del Rincón is a town with a livestock tradition with a beautiful urban complex and a mountain atmosphere in one of the most attractive areas for rural tourism in the Madrid mountains. At the entrance of the town there is a comfortable car park to leave vehicles for the two or three days that it may take to return in bikepacking mode to the Ayllón massif through the Somosierra pass.
La Puebla, a port with two slopes, has a rough start but before the second kilometer it softens.

Puebla de la Sierra is one of the black towns of Madrid, in the border area with Guadalajara. Black towns are so called because of their slate-built houses. This town is especially picturesque and its location on the mountainside, in a privileged setting, makes it an unforgettable visit.
This dam offers spectacular views. It is worth going there. Ideal for hiking, motorbike or road bike excursions. Next to the dam there is a small parking lot for about 10 cars.
